title: pycnandra amplexicaulis
intro: Pycnandra amplexicaulis, also known as the Moreton Bay cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sarcolataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Pacific Island medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pycnandra amplexicaulis include its ability to treat a range of conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In traditional medicine, Pycnandra amplexicaulis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it has also been used in the production of herbal remedies, including creams and ointments.
